description: CEO Arvind Krishna: 'I could easily see 30% of that getting replaced by AI and automation over a five-year period.' Paused hiring for ~26,000 non-customer-facing roles. ~7,800 roles expected to not be filled.
reasonGiven: CEO stated 30% of non-customer-facing roles could be replaced by AI over 5 years. AskHR chatbot handles 94% of routine HR queries.

description: New CEO Margherita Della Valle announced 11,000 cuts over three years to 'regain competitiveness.' Part of broader efficiency and technology modernization.
reasonGiven: CEO announced cuts to regain competitiveness through efficiency and technology modernization.

description: CEO Philip Jansen: ~10,000 roles to be replaced by AI; rest from fibre rollout completion. Called generative AI 'a significant leap forward.' BT will be 'a leaner business with a brighter future.'
reasonGiven: ~10,000 roles to be replaced by AI; remaining cuts from fibre rollout completion.

description: AI-driven productivity and automation. Earnings presentation cited 'workforce reductions, platform simplification' and 'adoption and facilitation of artificial intelligence.'
reasonGiven: AI-driven productivity and automation; workforce reductions and platform simplification.

description: Eliminate administrative positions as automation and AI rolled out. 'Profound changes by digitalisation and AI will lead to greater efficiency.'
reasonGiven: Profound changes by digitalisation and AI will lead to greater efficiency.

description: Restructuring plan to 'take full advantage of the possibilities offered by AI.' Up to 2,100 jobs impacted by 2027.
reasonGiven: Restructuring to take full advantage of AI possibilities.

description: To offset AI infrastructure spending (up to $135B capex in 2026). Reuters reported executives told senior leaders to begin preparing for cuts. Zuckerberg (Jan earnings call): 'We're starting to see projects that used to require big teams now be accomplished by a single very talented person.' Meta spokesperson: 'This is speculative reporting about theoretical approaches.'
reasonGiven: To offset AI infrastructure spending. Zuckerberg: 'Projects that used to require big teams now accomplished by a single very talented person.'
notes: Reported but unconfirmed. Meta spokesperson called it 'speculative reporting about theoretical approaches.'
